dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4527 IS: Improving Emerging Tech Opportunities for Veterans Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. McCormick (for himself and Mr. Gallego) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to provide greater opportunities for veterans to pursue education programs involving emerging technologies, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Improving Emerging Tech Opportunities for Veterans Act of 2026.

section.header: Inclusion of emerging technologies in high technology program

subsection.text: Section 3699C of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the section heading by striking High technology and inserting High technology and emerging technology;
by striking high technology and inserting high technology or emerging technology each place such term appears; and
in subsection (c)(4) by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Such criteria shall also identify which technologies of critical importance, such as artificial intelligence and semiconductor manufacturing, shall be treated as emerging technologies for purposes of this section.

subsection.text: The table of sections at the beginning of chapter 36 of such title is amended by striking the item relating to section 3699D and inserting the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: 3699C. High technology and emerging technology program.

subsection.text: Section 3680A of title 38, United States Code, is amended in each of subsections (a)(4)(B) and (d)(8) by striking high technology and inserting high technology or emerging technology.

section.header: Critical stakeholder partnerships for emerging technologies

subsection.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs, in partnership with critical stakeholders, shall—

paragraph.text: identify specific types of industries and occupations that, as a result of emerging technologies, are likely to have high employment and growth potential for individuals with appropriate education, training, or skills; and
identify courses of education that help veterans acquire the appropriate education, training, or skills to become employed in such industries and occupations.
The Secretary shall ensure that such emerging technologies, and such courses of education, industries, and occupations, are—

subparagraph.text: prominently displayed on the website of the Department of Veterans Affairs; and
promoted in transition services administered by the Secretary.

paragraph.text: The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that such emerging technologies, and such courses of education, industries, and occupations, are—

subparagraph.text: prominently displayed on the website of the Department of Defense; and
promoted in transition services administered by the Secretary of Defense.

paragraph.text: The Secretary of Labor shall ensure that such emerging technologies, and such courses of education, industries, and occupations, are promoted in transition services administered by the Secretary of Labor.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor—

paragraph.text: determine which technologies of critical importance, such as artificial intelligence and semiconductor manufacturing, shall be treated as emerging technologies for purposes of this section; and

subparagraph.text: update the list of industries and occupations identified in subsection (a)(1) as market conditions and demand evolve; and
use the updated list in carrying out subsection (b).

subsection.text: This section shall terminate on September 30, 2027.

In this section, the term critical stakeholders means persons in the private sector who have an interest in supporting veterans to pursue veteran education programs involving emerging technologies, such as employers in relevant industries, educational institutions offering relevant programs (whether or not leading to a standard college degree), and nonprofit organizations.
